skill.name: ai-architect-lite
skill.description: Lightweight workflow playbook for maintaining AI project context, mini plans, and append-only development logs with local Python helpers.
skill.summary: Guide AI-assisted projects with lightweight context files, planning templates, and development logs.
skill.author: Carl-312

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown backticks, documentation examples, Chinese text entropy, and security guidance that mentions secrets. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, shell spawning, or credential access was found. The confirmed risk is local filesystem modification through bundled helper scripts, which is legitimate for this workflow but should be disclosed to users.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Writes from Helper Scripts
medium_findings.description: The bundled helpers can create .ai_context/03_ACTIVE_TASK.md and write plan output to a user-selected path. This matches the skill purpose, but users should review paths before execution because local files can be created or overwritten.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly call mkdir and write_text on paths derived from command-line arguments. This is documented and intentional, but it is still a real local filesystem risk.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied Output Paths Need Review
medium_findings.description: The plan helper accepts an --out path and resolves it before writing. Its traversal check is not a strong project-root boundary, so a user-provided absolute path can target locations outside the current repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code shows arbitrary output path support and the security document acknowledges filesystem and sanitization limitations. No malicious intent is evident, so medium risk is appropriate.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, or command examples. I did not find code that invokes a shell, subprocess, eval, or dynamic execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Markdown formatting and local command examples, not executable shell calls inside the scripts. The Python files do not import subprocess or call shell APIs.

low_findings.title: Network and Credential Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The network match is contribution text, and the environment-file match is security guidance warning users not to commit secrets. I did not find network requests or credential reads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain documentation and warnings. Source review found no requests, urllib, socket usage, environment variable access, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Heuristics Are Caused by Multilingual Documentation
low_findings.description: The high entropy findings occur in Markdown files containing Chinese text, symbols, and examples. No encoded payload, binary blob, or obfuscated code was found in those files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection showed readable Markdown content rather than encrypted or packed data. The heuristic is plausibly triggered by multilingual text and formatting.

dangerous_patterns.title: File Creation and Overwrite APIs
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts use pathlib mkdir and write_text to create context files and write plan output. This is expected behavior, but it should remain visible to users because it changes local files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly performs local filesystem writes. The behavior is transparent and documented, so it is a warning rather than a blocker.

content.value_statement: AI-assisted projects can lose context across sessions and tasks. This skill provides lightweight memory files, mini planning, and local log helpers to keep work traceable.
actual_capabilities: Defines a lightweight .ai_context workflow for active task tracking.
Provides an append-only development log template for project work.
Includes a local Python helper to append structured log entries.
Includes a local Python helper to generate mini execution plans.
Documents slash-first dispatch and manifest-based workflow conventions.
Provides bilingual English and Chinese usage documentation.
limitations: Does not implement a full project management system.
Does not synchronize logs to remote services or databases.
Requires users to review file paths before running local helper scripts.
Does not validate user-provided log text for sensitive data.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap a small AI-assisted project
use_cases.description: Create a simple active task file and use a mini plan before editing project files.

use_cases.title: Keep session handoffs readable
use_cases.description: Record decisions, changes, outcomes, and next steps in a consistent local log.

use_cases.title: Port a lightweight workflow pattern
use_cases.description: Adapt the AI Architect memory and dispatch pattern without adopting a larger framework.

prompt_templates.title: Create a mini plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use AI Architect Lite to create a short plan for this goal: [goal]. Include constraints, three steps, and validation.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize task context
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up the lightweight .ai_context active task workflow for this repository. Explain each file change before making it.

prompt_templates.title: Append a development log
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a structured development log entry for this work: [summary]. Include changes, outcome, and next step.

prompt_templates.title: Audit workflow readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repository for AI Architect Lite readiness. Check active task context, logging practices, manifest usage, and safety gaps.

output_examples.input: Plan a small authentication task with validation.
output_examples.output: A concise mini plan with goal, constraints, up to five steps, and a verification checklist.

output_examples.input: Record that the README and tests were updated.
output_examples.output: A timestamped development log entry with action, changes, outcome, and next step.

output_examples.input: Prepare an existing repository for lightweight AI context tracking.
output_examples.output: A brief setup recommendation covering active task notes, logging, and manifest dispatch conventions.

best_practices: Review the target path before running either helper script.
Keep active task notes concise and free of secrets.
Record validation results after each meaningful change.
anti_patterns: Do not store API keys, credentials, or private tokens in .ai_context files.
Do not run helper scripts with elevated privileges unless required and reviewed.
Do not treat generated plans as a substitute for testing.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides a lightweight workflow for project context, planning, and development logging during AI-assisted work.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No. The included helpers use Python standard library features and operate on local files.

faq.question: Can it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: The documentation suggests local Python helper commands, but the scripts themselves do not spawn shell commands.

faq.question: Where does it write data?
faq.answer: The log helper writes to .ai_context/03_ACTIVE_TASK.md under the selected project root.

faq.question: Is it suitable for teams?
faq.answer: Yes, for small teams that want a simple shared convention for AI task context and logs.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing logs?
faq.answer: Users should check that logs do not include secrets, credentials, private customer data, or unrelated file paths.
